domain.title: Service Dispatch
domain.description: Operational knowledge for a small home-services dispatch team covering scheduling, deposits, emergency jobs, client exceptions, and approval flows.

authority_policy.description: Signed customer policy, contract addendum, or owner-approved written policy.
Internal standard operating procedure or dispatch playbook.
Written guidance from operations lead or department manager.
Interview-derived guidance from the lead dispatcher or founder.
Unstructured notes with no stronger authority.

sample_questions: Can we waive the deposit for this emergency call?
Who needs to approve after-hours scheduling?
What is different for long-term maintenance clients?
When can emergency requests bypass normal scheduling rules?

sample_objects.title: After-hours approval required
sample_objects.canonical_statement: After-hours scheduling requires operations lead approval unless the request is classified as emergency priority.

sample_objects.title: Emergency after-hours bypass
sample_objects.canonical_statement: Emergency requests may bypass normal after-hours approval if safety, active damage, or service outage criteria are met.

sample_objects.title: Deposit waiver flow
sample_objects.canonical_statement: Deposit waivers require checking client category, emergency status, and approval path before scheduling confirmation.
